title: Cloud service credentials must be confirmed and kept out of firmware
symptoms: Generated firmware contains a real API key or token
Deploy starts before user has created a provider API key
A paid cloud provider is selected without official setup links
Async firmware blocks while calling a synchronous cloud API
wrong_pattern: Hard-code provider secrets or silently assume the user has enabled a paid cloud API.
correct_pattern: Ask the user to confirm provider and credential readiness, provide official links, store only secret names/placeholders, and block deploy-ready success until cloud setup is resolved.
fix_guidance: Load knowledge/cloud_service_catalog.json and present provider docs/console/pricing links.
Move real secrets to deploy-time prompts or a non-committed device secrets file.
Use custom_http_proxy when provider signing, OAuth, or account secrets are unsuitable for firmware.
Set next_phase to simulate/null when cloud setup is mock-only or blocked.
